system_description.overview: Clado is an AI-powered people search and discovery platform that aggregates professional profiles from LinkedIn, GitHub, and Google Scholar into a ground-truth dataset. It uses advanced ranking models and reinforcement learning pipelines ('LLM as judge') to deliver agentic search over structured and unstructured data. Products power hyper-personalized outreach, reference checks, and research automation.

products.People Data Platform: Aggregation, normalization, and enrichment of professional data from multiple public sources with API and platform integration for recruiting, sales, and enterprise systems
products.AI-Powered Search & Discovery: Semantic and structured search optimized for people data; advanced ranking models and RL pipelines for relevance
products.Reference & Research Tools: AI-assisted reference checking, automated research reports, enrichment of profiles with contextual signals
products.Enterprise Integration: API and platform access for integration into recruiting, sales, and enterprise systems

infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (spins up multiple servers across all AZs on availability zone failure)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: VPC with segmented subnets: Public subnet (bastion EC2), App Private subnet (EC2 instances), Data Private subnet (RDS MySQL, RDS Postgres, OpenSearch). NAT Gateway in public subnet for outbound-only private subnet traffic. S3 VPC Endpoint for private S3 access. ACLs and security groups enforced.
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services; reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN; remote access via bastion EC2 in public subnet, SSH from office IP only (2/tcp from Office IP via SSH)
network_architecture.tls: TLS via established third-party certificate authority for all web-based communications
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; all systems restricted to internet via proxy
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> Internet Gateway -> Bastion EC2 (public subnet) -> App Private subnet (EC2) and Data Private subnet (RDS MySQL, RDS Postgres, OpenSearch Index). NAT Gateway for outbound. S3 Buckets via VPC Endpoint.

application_architecture.pattern: Not explicitly stated; likely Microservices/Hybrid given separate compute, search index, and dual-DB setup
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments (CC8.1.4: changes tested in separate dev/test environment before implementation)

data_storage.backup_frequency: Formal backup policy in place; frequency not explicitly stated

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (passwords controlled through AWS Password policy)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code tool access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: AWS Password policy: periodic forced changes, complexity requirements, account lockout after failed attempts; no guest/anonymous logins; inactivity screen lock

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (AWS KMS implied by CC6.1.5: 'uses its cloud provider key management service to encrypt data at rest')

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (referred to as 'source code management tool'; MFA-protected)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to production (CC8.1.2)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback; baseline configs retained as code (CC7.1.1)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations auto-update and scanned daily for compliance; critical/security patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring tool (unnamed) for capacity and performance; generates alerts on predefined thresholds
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, function changes, security config changes at app and infra level; log access is restricted
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts to IT management for high and critical risk events

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure, data center, compute, storage, databases, networking

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software on all production servers (auto-scan continuous, definitions updated weekly) and Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; high/critical remediated via remediation plan
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to data center facilities excluded; AWS is responsible for physical security
A1.2 - Environmental protections excluded; Clado does not have office/working space; AWS manages environmental controls
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria - determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.3: Source code changes logged, time-stamped, and attributed in source control; access restricted via MFA
CC6.2.2: Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged
CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
CC7.1.2: Monthly automated vulnerability scans with remediation plans
CC7.4.1: BCDR policy documented, reviewed, and tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Section 5: DR achieved by spinning up multiple servers across all AWS availability zones on AZ failure

infrastructure.rationale: Single region (us-east-1) with multi-AZ failover. VPC with proper segmentation, bastion host, IDS/IPS. No CDN, no IaC tool named (config-as-code implied). AWS carve-out.

application_architecture.rationale: Architecture pattern not explicitly disclosed. EC2-based compute visible in network diagram. Languages/frameworks not named. Dual DB (MySQL + Postgres) plus OpenSearch suggests non-trivial architecture. Branch protection and separate envs confirmed.

data_layer.rationale: Two RDS databases (MySQL + Postgres) plus OpenSearch index and S3. Multi-AZ. Data classification and retention policies in place. No caching layer or data warehouse mentioned. Backup policy exists but frequency not stated.

security_posture.rationale: IDS/IPS via AWS, firewalls, annual external pen testing, monthly vuln scans, AV on all servers, MFA enforced for admin access, quarterly access reviews, KMS encryption at rest, TLS in transit. No WAF or SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection confirmed, separate environments, emergency change process, config-as-code implied but IaC tool not named. CI/CD tool not named. No containers mentioned.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, annually reviewed and tested across multiple loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover via spinning up servers across AZs. No RTO/RPO disclosed. No multi-region.

vendor_diversity.rationale: AWS is the sole named subservice organization. No other cloud or third-party vendors disclosed. Heavy single-vendor dependency on AWS.

overall.rationale: Clado has solid foundational security controls for a SOC 2 Type 1 startup: multi-AZ AWS, proper VPC segmentation, external pen testing, MFA, quarterly access reviews. Gaps include single region, no CDN/WAF named, unnamed CI/CD toolchain, no disclosed RTO/RPO, and near-exclusive AWS dependency.

red_flags: Single AWS region only (us-east-1); no multi-region DR
No RTO or RPO disclosed anywhere in the report
No WAF mentioned despite web-facing platform handling sensitive professional data
Heavy single-vendor dependency: AWS is the only named subservice organization
CI/CD tooling, source control platform, and programming languages not disclosed
yellow_flags: Backup frequency not explicitly stated (policy exists but schedule undisclosed)
No CDN mentioned for a platform aggregating large-scale professional data
No SIEM named; logging appears manual/alert-based rather than centralized
IaC tool not named (config-as-code implied but tool unspecified)
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Monthly automated vulnerability scans with remediation plans
Multi-AZ failover explicitly described in Section 5 DR narrative
Quarterly access reviews for all accounts including privileged
MFA enforced for admin access and source code tool access
Access revocation within one business day of termination
KMS encryption at rest; TLS in transit
Network diagram provided showing full VPC topology with bastion, private subnets, data subnet
BCDR tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Branch protection: merge to production requires additional approval
Dual database engines (MySQL + Postgres) plus OpenSearch search index — purpose-fit data layer
key_observations: The product name 'Clado' is a brand name; legal entity is Linkd Inc. — distinct from LinkedIn
System scope title in auditor's report is 'Agent tool context infrastructure' — suggests agent/AI orchestration layer is a core infra component
OpenSearch index alongside dual RDS suggests semantic search capability directly in production data subnet, not delegated to a third-party search API
The RL pipeline ('LLM as judge') is a notable technical differentiator disclosed in system description but no AI/ML infrastructure vendors named
No mention of Anthropic, OpenAI, or any LLM API vendor — either self-hosted models or vendors deliberately excluded from scope
Data source aggregation from LinkedIn, GitHub, Google Scholar raises potential ToS/legal exposure not addressed in controls
